arguments.task: Open and read official DataForSEO documentation pages that establish: 1) what the product/API does, 2) documented authentication methods for the API, 3) whether the API uses REST and/or GraphQL, 4) pricing or access/signup information that shows whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/gated, 5) rate limit information, and 6) whether there is any official MCP server or MCP documentation. Prefer canonical first-party docs pages and pricing pages. Return the exact page URLs visited and concise extracted findings with quotes or paraphrases tied to each URL. Do not rely on third-party sites or search snippets.

input.thought: Open official DataForSEO docs and extract supported research fields from canonical first-party pages.

arguments.task: Open only official first-party DataForSEO pages and extract final evidence with canonical URLs and supporting text for these fields: product description, authentication methods, API protocol style, rate limits, and official MCP availability. Visit the docs homepage, the authentication page, any introduction/general/API overview page with request examples or endpoint structure, and any official page mentioning rate limits. Also check DataForSEO's official site for pricing/free trial or signup. Return a concise structured summary with exact URLs and the supporting text found on each page. If a field is not found on first-party pages, explicitly say not found.

input.thought: Run a narrower second browser pass to extract exact official evidence and canonical URLs for DataForSEO.

raw_result.description: API platform for SEO, SERP, keyword, backlink, and on-page data retrieval and analysis.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official authentication documentation describes HTTP Basic Authentication using your DataForSEO login and password, encoded in Base64 in the Authorization header. No other first-party API auth method was verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official pricing page presents self-serve signup and a 'Try for free' option, indicating developers can start without sales or admin approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public v3 API documentation with multiple resource groups for SEO/SERP-related data; browser evidence verified REST usage but did not fully enumerate the entire surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or MCP documentation was found in the gathered first-party evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit details were not verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are publicly accessible; pricing appears pay-as-you-go with a free-try entry point and a $50 minimum payment noted during browser extraction.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured field api_surface.mcp_exists could not be strongly supported by a dedicated official MCP page in the gathered evidence, and rate-limit information was not located. All other structured fields were supported by the visited official pages.
